_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**SmartWay and Scope 3 accounting:** SmartWay's emissions accounting methodology is built on EPA-developed emissions factors for freight transportation modes. Its role in Scope 3 Category 4 (upstream transportation and distribution) accounting is recognized within U.S. corporate sustainability reporting practice. EPA's transportation GHG data supports the methodology's emissions factor foundation.

*Source: EPA Fast Facts on Transportation Greenhouse Gas Emissions, U.S. Environmental Protection Agency. https://www.epa.gov/greenvehicles/fast-facts-transportation-greenhouse-gas-emissions*

**Global programs modeled on SmartWay:** EPA's SmartWay Global Collaboration page (linked from the program homepage) documents international freight programs that have been modeled on SmartWay, including initiatives in Canada, Japan, and other jurisdictions. For a workspace with Americas, Europe, and Asia trade lane exposure, understanding which trade lanes have SmartWay-equivalent programs is a research gap worth closing — particularly as European clients subject to CSRD may require emissions accounting that is not satisfied by U.S. SmartWay enrollment alone. No authoritative cross-program equivalency guidance has been identified in the sources available for this brief.

*Source: EPA SmartWay Program (Global Collaboration section), U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, last updated April 14, 2026. https://www.epa.gov/smartway*

**EPA Heavy-Duty Vehicle GHG Phase 3:** As new Phase 3-compliant vehicles enter the carrier fleet from model year 2027 onward, SmartWay carrier performance benchmarks will shift. Workspaces using SmartWay emissions data for client Scope 3 reporting should anticipate that the same carriers may show improved per-ton-mile emissions profiles as fleet renewal occurs — a commercially positive development for those clients' emissions inventories.

*Source: Federal Register, EPA, April 22, 2024. https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2024/04/22/2024-06809/greenhouse-gas-emissions-standards-for-heavy-duty-vehicles-phase-3*
